data.title: Stock market opens higher as Waller hints at potential rate cuts from July
data.description:
The stock market inched up on Friday, as Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller hinted at potential rate cuts in July. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.5%, the Dow (DJI) +0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.95%.

Today also marks the second triple witching event of the year—a day on which $6.5T worth of contracts tied to stock options, stock index futures and stock index options will expire. Such days have typically seen volatility in equity markets.

Waller said Friday he sees little risk that tariffs will stoke inflation and signaled the central bank could start cutting interest rates as soon as July.

President Donald Trump is expected to decide within two weeks on a possible U.S. strike on Iran amid the latter's conflict with Israel. Investors took some relief from this after talks of the U.S. entering the tensions hurt sentiments. 

"Markets are recovering a bit this morning after White House spokeswoman Leavitt last night said that Trump had dictated a message saying that 'based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks,'" Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

That means two weeks of uncertainty for financial markets, but investors are still inclined to see the Middle East conflict as a local, not a global, economic issue, UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Wall Street on Wednesday erased modest gains to end little changed, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said uncertainty stemming from tariffs continued to call for a "wait-and-see" approach to monetary policy actions.

The Fed left its policy rate at 4.25%-4.50% on Wednesday for a fourth straight meeting. 

The U.S. stock market was closed on Thursday to observe Juneteenth, after the NYSE and Nasdaq adopted the day as a new market holiday in 2022.

Turning to the economic side, the Philly Fed Manufacturing gauge stalled at -4 in June, missing consensus.

The May U.S. Leading Index will be released during market hours and is forecasted to come in at -0.1% M/M.

The Fed's balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Apollo-backed Athora in talks for acquisition of UK-based Pension Insurance Corporation - report
data.description:
Apollo (NYSE:APO)-backed savings and retirement services group, Athora, is in discussions to acquire control of the specialist UK insurer Pension Insurance Corporation, Sky News reported on Friday.

Bankers estimate the deal could be worth between £4B and £5B, representing a landmark transaction in the pension risk transfer market, the report noted.

The details about the potential deal, including its pricing and structure, were reportedly unclear.

The U.S. private equity firm was said to have been considering a bid for PIC in 2023 as well, along with KKR (KKR) and Carlyle Group (CG).

data.title: Robin Energy stock slides after pricing $4M offering
data.description:
• Shares of Robin Energy (NASDAQ:RBNE) fell over 30% premarket on Friday after the shipping company priced a stock offering to raise around $4M.
  • The offering consists of 763,000 common shares, issued at a price of $5.25.
  • Robin Energy currently intends to use the net proceeds from the sale for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about June 20, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FinVolution Group prices $130M convertible notes offering
data.description:
FinVolution Group (NYSE:FINV) has pricing of its offering of convertible senior notes in an aggregate principal amount of US$130M due 2030.

The notes will bear interest at 2.50% per year, payable semi-annually, and will mature on July 1, 2030 unless repurchased, redeemed, or converted. The initial conversion rate is 80.8865 ADSs per US$1,000 principal amount of the notes, which is equivalent to an initial conversion price of ~$12.36 per ADS.

The company has granted the initial purchasers an option to purchase up to an additional $20M of the notes, exercisable for settlement within a 13-day period beginning on, and including, the date on which the notes are first issued.

FinVolution plans to use the net proceeds from the offering for international business expansions, replenishment of working capital, and funding the concurrent repurchase of 6.37M ADSs from certain purchasers of the notes.

The offering is expected to close on or about June 24, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Source: Press Release

data.title: ArcelorMittal nixes plans for two green steel plants in Germany due to high energy costs
data.description:
ArcelorMittal (NYSE:MT) said it canceled plans to convert two plants in Germany to carbon-neutral steel production because the country's energy costs were too high, Reuters reported Friday, in the latest blow to Germany's industrial sector.

The German government had hoped €1.3B in subsidies would encourage ArcelorMittal (NYSE:MT) to convert existing plants at Bremen in the north and Eisenhuettenstadt in the east to use furnaces fired with hydrogen, which can be created from renewably generated electricity.

But the company said it will not move forward with the plans because energy costs in Germany were too high and there was too much uncertainty about the country's future energy mix.

"The first electric arc forges are being built in countries that can offer competitive and predictable electricity provision," ArcelorMittal (MT) said, noting its recent investment in an electricity-fueled forge in nuclear-powered France.

"Electricity prices in Germany are high both by international standards and compared to neighbouring countries," the company also said.

Three other projects - by Salzgitter, Thyssenkrupp and Stahl-Holding-Saar - remain under way, the German economy ministry said.

data.title: Madrigal, Ipsen, SpringWorks get EU backing for their drugs
data.description:
Madrigal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:MDGL), Exelixis partner Ipsen (NASDAQ:EXEL), and SpringWorks Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SWTX) said that an advisor of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed their respective treatments.

Issuing a so-called positive opinion, EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has recommended the marketing authorization for Madrigal's Rezdiffra, a liver-directed THR-β agonist, for the treatment of adults with noncirrhotic MASH with moderate to advanced liver fibrosis.

The EU decision on Rezdiffra is expected in August 2025.

The CHMP has also backed the approval of Ipsen's Cabometyx to treat patients with previously treated advanced neuroendocrine tumors. The regulatory decision for the drug is anticipated in 2025.

SpringWorks said that the CHMP suggested allowing conditional marketing approval for its MEK inhibitor, Mirdametinib, to treat symptomatic, inoperable plexiform neurofibromas in children and adults with neurofibromatosis type 1 who are 2 years old and older.

The EU decision for Mirdametinib is expected in the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Couchbase stock soars 28% premarket on $1.5B all-cash buyout by Haveli Investments
data.description:
Couchbase (NASDAQ:BASE) shares surged +28% in premarket trading Friday after announcing a deal to be acquired by Haveli Investments, a tech-focused investment firm, in an all-cash transaction valued at approximately $1.5 billion.

Couchbase (NASDAQ:BASE) stockholders to receive $24.50 per share in cash.

The deal offers a 67% premium to Couchbase’s March 27 closing price and a 29% premium to its June 18 close.

"The data layer in enterprise IT stacks is continuing to increase in importance as a critical enabler of next-gen AI applications," said Sumit Pande, Senior Managing Director at Haveli Investments.

"Couchbase's innovative data platform is well positioned to meet the performance and scalability demands of the largest global enterprises."

Source: Press Release

data.title: Tesla opening showrooms in India next month
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) is finally entering the Indian market, opening a showroom in Mumbai and New Delhi, selling Chinese-made Model Y SUVs in the world’s most populated country.

The Mumbai showroom is scheduled to open in mid-July, followed later in the month with a showroom in New Delhi, Bloomberg reported on Friday. The milestone comes just 5 months after Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) CEO Elon Musk met with Indian prime minister Narendra Modi to discuss the country’s trade policies and manufacturing hurdles. India assesses a 70% tax on fully-built imported vehicles under $40K

According to documents viewed by Bloomberg, five Model Y vehicles have already arrived in Mumbai from Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) Shanghai factory. The vehicles were declared at 2.77M rupees ($32K) and attracted more than 2.1M rupees in import fees. This puts the vehicle at a base sticker price of more than $56K versus ~$40K for the same vehicle in the U.S., making it a tough sell in a country in which EVs account for just 5% of new vehicle sales, and less than 2% for luxury vehicles.

Nonetheless, the company is planning to expand throughout the country, with warehouses planned for Karnataka and Gurugram.

In March 2024, the Indian government introduced policies to increase the adoption of electric vehicles and attract global investment in its manufacturing sector. Under the new policy, up to 8,000 EVs can be imported annually by a foreign auto manufacturer at a reduced tariff rate, but only if the company commits $500M to establish local manufacturing facilities and component sourcing within the country.

Accordingly, Tesla (TSLA) has reportedly submitted a proposal for a $2B to $3B facility that can manufacture up to 500K vehicles per year.

data.title: Darden Restaurants increases dividend by ~7% to $1.50
data.description:
• Darden Restaurants (NYSE:DRI) declares $1.50/share quarterly dividend, 7.1% increase from prior dividend of $1.40.
  • Forward yield 2.69%
  • Payable Aug. 1; for shareholders of record July 10; ex-div July 10.
  • See DRI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Analysts say Netflix starting small with live TV before going big is 'no surprise'
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) treading cautiously when trying new things is not an unfamiliar move.

The streaming giant testing the waters in France with live TV is the latest example. If successful, the company will further grow its subscribers and make traditional broadcasting obsolete over time globally.

Max Greve, a Seeking Alpha analyst, commented on the live TV initiative with TF1, saying Netflix "usually experiments in medium-sized markets before global rollouts."

Starting in the summer of 2026, the company is expected to carry between five and nine TV channels for its 13.6M subscribers in France, which doesn't appear to be overwhelming given the headway it has made in its livestreaming technologies.

The French TV group currently offers five free-to-air channels: TF1, TMC, TFX, TF1 Séries Films, and LCI; and four paid channels: TV Breizh, Histoire TV, Ushuaïa TV, and Serieclub.

Greve thinks Netflix could "definitely" expand to the U.S. and other places if the reception is positive in France. The U.S. market, which is also the company's biggest, is nearly six times the size of France, boasting 81.4M subscribers.

"There is both potential and risk here, because a livestream means that control over curation shifts from Netflix to the one providing the live feed," Greve said. "Netflix is continuing to push into live content because even ad-free subscribers watch ads on a live broadcast, and their ad business remains subscale."  

The Los Gatos, California-based company has transformed from being a movie rental business into a household streaming name. Commenting on the company's entry into the live TV market, Seeking Alpha analyst The Entertainment Oracle said Netflix realized over the years that "they have to adapt to stay a leader" in the media field.

The company's quiet entry comes at a time when television viewership via streaming has outpaced both cable and broadcast combined for the first time ever in the U.S.

Netflix has gone from curating original content for linear channels to hosting linear channels and external content, The Entertainment Oracle pointed out. "This represents a major industry shift that we'll likely see them try to duplicate down the line," the analyst said.

"It’s not a surprise Netflix would look to test this type of partnership in a market like France," the analyst said. "You may remember a few years ago, they rolled out the FAST-centric “Netflix Direct” initiative in the same location. This has essentially expanded that concept out in a substantial way."
